xEnrich 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference

xEnrich: Multi-Scale Enrichment with Information Theory and Location Context

Source: R/xEnrich-package.R
xEnrich-package.Rd

A unified framework for translating omics results into biological context. Provides two complementary analysis tracks: (1) an information-theoretic enrichment track that scores gene sets by mutual information rather than raw overlap, uses adaptive permutation testing with explicit confidence intervals on p-values, and identifies the minimal non-redundant set of independent signals via greedy conditional mutual information selection; and (2) a location-aware track that maps gene or protein lists to enriched organs using Human Protein Atlas data (IHC and RNA) and then resolves cell-type origin within those organs using Tabula Sapiens single-cell transcriptomes. The two tracks share a common gene-list interface and can be combined to answer not only which pathways are altered but where in the body and in which cell type the changes are most relevant. Reference data (Human Protein Atlas and Tabula Sapiens) is hosted on Zenodo (https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19106109) and cached locally on first use.

See also
